title: Spouse Relief/Spouse Relief (Disability)
text:
Spouse Relief/Spouse Relief (Disability) - SupportGoWhere
Spouse Relief/Spouse Relief (Disability)
Share link
Provides tax relief of up to $5,500 to both male and female taxpayers who have supported their spouses.
SCHEME HIGHLIGHTS
Spouse Relief of $2,000, up to $5,500 for spouse with disability or mental impairment
Apply now
Collapse all sections
About this support

Spouse Relief of $2,000 for taxpayers who have supported their spouses

Spouse Relief (Disability) of $5,500 for taxpayers who have supported their spouses with disabilities

Who is this for?

For Spouse Relief:

You must fulfil all these conditions in the previous year:

Your spouse has been living with / supported by you

Your spouse did not have an annual income of more than $8,000

You have made maintenance payments under a Court Order or Deed of Separation (if you are legally separated from your spouse)



For Spouse Relief (Disability):

Dependant must be certified by a doctor to require the need for assistance with any of the 6 Activities of Daily Living:

Washing or Bathing

Dressing

Feeding

Toileting

Transferring

Mobility

Dependant must be certified by a doctor to be impaired in any of these 3 areas:

Self-Care and Activities of Daily Living

Compliance to Psychiatric Treatment

Education or Work

No other claimants may claim the Parent Relief / Parent Relief (Disability) or Sibiling Relief (Disability) on the same dependant (your spouse)

How to apply?

For e-Filing

Is this the first time you are claiming the relief?



Yes

	

No




Log in with Singpass or Singpass Foreign user Account(SFA) at myTax Portal

Go to “Individuals” > “File Income Tax Return”

Select “File” > File Income Tax Return

Go to “4. Deductions, Tax Reliefs and Rebates”

Select “Add New” > “Spouse”

Fill in the relevant information and select “Update”

	

If you were previously approved for relief last year, the amount will be pre-filled and granted to you automatically

Follow the steps on the left and select “Not Claiming” if you wish to withdraw your claim





For Paper Filing

Is this the first time you are claiming the relief?




Yes

	

No




Complete the item on “Spouse/Spouse Relief (Disability)” section on Form B1 & B

Complete the particulars of your spouse in Appendix 2

	

Complete the item on “Spouse/Spouse Relief (Disability)” section on Form B1 & B





For first-time Handicapped Spouse Relief claims:

Email 1 of the following documents via myTax Mail

Completed Application for Claim of Disability Related Tax Reliefs

Letter / supporting documents from the disability association in respect of your spouse, indicating details of their disabilities

Where can I find help?

View more details

IRAS hotline: 1800-356-8300

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

Submit an enquiry

Visit your nearest branch

Scheme last updated 23 Sep 2025
